perturbation.original_text: 4. Notice of Pending Th ird-Party Disclosure or Unauthorized Disclosure. (a) In the event that any governmental authority issues an order, subpoena, or other lawful process or a Participating Entity receives a discovery request in a civil proc eeding ("Legal Process") requiring the disclosure of any Confidential Information, the Participating Entity recei ving such Legal Process shall notify in writing the other Participating Entities within five (5) business days of receipt. The Participating Entity receiving such Legal Process shal l not be in violation of this Agreement if it complies with the Legal Process re quiring disclosure of the Confid ential Information after seven (7) business days following Participating En tity notification, as set forth above.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. Notice of Pending Th ird-Party Disclosure or Unauthorized Disclosure. (a) In the event that any governmental authority issues an order, subpoena, or other lawful process or a Participating Entity receives a discovery request in a civil proc eeding ("Legal Process") requiring the disclosure of any Confidential Information, the Participating Entity recei ving such Legal Process shall notify in writing the other Participating Entities within five (5) business days of receipt. The Participating Entity receiving such Legal Process shal l not be in violation of this Agreement if it complies with the Legal Process re quiring disclosure of the Confid ential Information immediately following Participating En tity notification, as set forth above.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by stating that compliance with legal process requiring disclosure is acceptable immediately after notification, contradicting the original clause that allowed a seven (7) business day period before compliance. This change creates an in-text contradiction regarding the permitted timeframe for disclosure.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. Notice of Pending Th ird-Party Disclosure or Unauthorized Disclosure. (a) In the event that any governmental authority issues an order, subpoena, or other lawful process or a Participating Entity receives a discovery request in a civil proc eeding ("Legal Process") requiring the disclosure of any Confidential Information, the Participating Entity recei ving such Legal Process shall notify in writing the other Participating Entities within five (5) business days of receipt. The Participating Entity receiving such Legal Process shal l not be in violation of this Agreement if it complies with the Legal Process re quiring disclosure of the Confid ential Information immediately following Participating En tity notification, as set forth above. (b) A Participating Entity shall not disclose any Confidential Info rmation in response to a request under the federal Freedom of Information Act, 5 U.S. C. § 552, as amended, or an equivalent state or local open records law, except as required by law as determined in the written opinion of such Participating Entity’s legal counsel. Upon receipt of a Freedom of Information Act or public records disclosure request, such Participating Entity shall: (i) notify each Participating Entity or Participating Entities whose information is subject to such discl osure request immediately upon receipt of a request for public records that includ e all or part of the Confidential Information; and (ii) if, in the written opinion of the legal counsel for the Partici pating Entity receiving the information request, the Confidential Information is not legally required to be disclosed, treat the requested Confidential Information as exempt from disclo sure to the extent permitted by applicable law. The Participating Entity receiving the information r equest shall cooperate with the Part icipating Entity or Participating Entities whose information is subject to such disclosure request in challenging the request or seeking another appropriate remedy, as necessary. If such challenge to the request is not successful and another remedy is not obtained, on ly that portion of the Confidential Information that is legally required to be disclosed, as determined in the written opinion of the Particip ating Entity’s legal counsel, shall be disclosed. (c) Unauthorized Disclosure: If a Participating Entity becomes aware that Confidential Information has been or likely has been disclosed to a third party in violation of this Agreement, the Participating Entity will immediately notify the Participating Entity in writing that provided the disclosed Confidential Information, provide a description of th e information disclosed, and provide reasonable assistance to the Participating En tity that provided the disclosed Co nfidential Information to recover the Confidential Information and prevent further unauthorized disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Section 4(a) to state that a Participating Entity can comply with legal process requiring disclosure of Confidential Information 'immediately following Participating Entity notification.' The contradicted text contains the original wording of Section 4(a), but because the changed text now says 'immediately', the original seven-day window is removed. This creates a direct and irreconcilable conflict regarding the timeframe for compliance, thereby resulting in a strong, in-text contradiction as defined by 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Term. This Agreement shall remain in effect as to each Participating Entity unless and until a Participating Entity seeking to withd raw from the agreement provides ten (1 0) days’ prior written notice to the other Participating Entities, then this Agreemen t shall terminate with re spect to such Participating Entity at the conclusion of such ten (10) day period; provided, however, that termination shall not extinguish any claim, liability, or cause of action under this Agreement existing at the time of termination.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Term. This Agreement shall remain in effect as to each Participating Entity unless and until a Participating Entity seeking to withd raw from the agreement provides ten (1 0) days’ prior written notice to the other Participating Entities, then this Agreemen t shall terminate with re spect to such Participating Entity immediately upon receipt of notice; provided, however, that termination shall not extinguish any claim, liability, or cause of action under this Agreement existing at the time of termination.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction in the 'Term' section. The original text stipulates a ten (10) day notice period before termination becomes effective. By changing the text to indicate immediate termination upon receipt of notice, the text contradicts its earlier statement regarding the notice period. This creates ambiguity about when termination actually occurs.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Term. This Agreement shall remain in effect as to each Participating Entity unless and until a Participating Entity seeking to withd raw from the agreement provides ten (1 0) days’ prior written notice to the other Participating Entities, then this Agreemen t shall terminate with re spect to such Participating Entity immediately upon receipt of notice; provided, however, that termination shall not extinguish any claim, liability, or cause of action under this Agreement existing at the time of termination. The provisions of S ections 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 and 6 shall survive the termination of this Agreement for a period of ten (10) years.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 5 modifies the termination clause from a ten (10) day notice period to immediate termination upon receipt of notice. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' which references the same section (Section 5) and also states that 'The provisions of Sections 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 and 6 shall survive the termination of this Agreement for a period of ten (10) years'. The original text and contradicted text both state that there is a 10 day notice period before the agreement is terminated, while the changed text states that the agreement is terminated immediately upon notice. This is a strong in-text contradiction as it creates uncertainty about when termination actually occurs, and contradicts whether or not Section 5 survives for 10 years after termination.

perturbation.original_text: 6. Return or Destruction of Confidential Informat ion. Upon termination of this Agreement, all Confidential Information in the possession or contro l of a Participating Entity and its Representatives that received such information shall be returned to the Participating Entity that disclosed the information, including all copies of such info rmation in any form whatsoever, unless otherwise instructed in writing by the Participating Entity tha t disclosed the information.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. Return or Destruction of Confidential Informat ion. Upon termination of this Agreement, all Confidential Information in the possession or contro l of a Participating Entity and its Representatives that received such information shall be retained indefinitely by the Participating Entity, regardless of the original disclosing party's instructions.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by changing the obligation to return or destroy confidential information to an obligation to retain it indefinitely. This contradicts the section's original intent and creates ambiguity about how confidential information should be handled after termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. Return or Destruction of Confidential Informat ion. Upon termination of this Agreement, all Confidential Information in the possession or contro l of a Participating Entity and its Representatives that received such information shall be retained indefinitely by the Participating Entity, regardless of the original disclosing party's instructions. Notwithstanding the foregoing, if the Confidential Information is retain ed in the computer backup system of a Participating Final June 2016 4 Entity, the Confidential Information will be destroye d in accordance with the regular ongoing records retention process of the Participating Entity. In lieu of return, a Partic ipating Entity may certify to the other Participating Entities in writ ing that all such Confidential Info rmation, in any form whatsoever, has been destroyed. Notwithstanding anything in this paragraph 6 to th e contrary, a Participating Entity may retain a record copy of any Confidential Informatio n if required to do so by applicable law. In such an instance, such Participatin g Entity shall identify in writing the specific Confidential Information retained, and shall provide the affected Participating Enti ty or Participating Entities with a written commitment to return or destroy the retained Confidenti al Information upon the expiration of the retention period required by law. The obligation un der this Agreement to main tain the confidentiality of all Confidential Information shall continue to app ly to such retained Confidential Information for so long as the Participating Entity posse sses such Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text mandates indefinite retention of confidential information, regardless of the disclosing party's instructions. The contradicted text acknowledges this indefinite retention but includes exceptions for destruction according to record retention processes, the option for certification of destruction in lieu of return, and retention as required by law with specific conditions for eventual return or destruction. These exceptions directly contradict the 'regardless of the original disclosing party's instructions' clause in the changed text, creating a strong in-text contradiction regarding the permissible handling of confidential information upon termination of the agreement as according to the explanation provided.

perturbation.original_text: 7. Notices. All notices, requests, demands, and other communications required or permitted under this Agreement shall be in writ ing, unless otherwise agreed by th e Participating Entities, and shall be delivered in person or sent by certified mail, postage prepaid, by overnight deliv ery, or by electronic mail or electronic facsimile transmission with an original sent immediately thereafter by postage prepaid mail, and properly addressed with r espect to a particular Participating Entity, to such Participating Entity’s representative as set forth on such Participating Entity’s signature page to this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. Notices. All notices, requests, demands, and other communications required or permitted under this Agreement must be communicated verbally to each Participating Entity.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by changing the method of providing notices. The original text specifies that notices must be in writing and delivered through specific methods. By changing the text to require verbal communication, the text directly contradicts its earlier statement about written notices. This creates ambiguity about how notices should be given and received.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. Notices. All notices, requests, demands, and other communications required or permitted under this Agreement must be communicated verbally to each Participating Entity. A Participating Entity may from time to time cha nge its representative or address for the purpose of notices to that Participating Entity by a simi lar notice specifying a new representative or address, but no such change shall be deemed to have been given until such notice is actually received by the Participating Entity being so notified.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that notices must be in writing, while the changed text dictates that all notices must be communicated verbally. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, therefore there is a contradiction, since the original text and contradicted text are opposing on whether notices should be written or verbal. This creates a direct and irreconcilable conflict within the contract, which is an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 9. No Warranties or Representations. Any Conf idential Information disclosed under this Agreement carries no warranty or representation of any kind, either express or implied. A Participating Entity receiving such Confidential Information shal l not be entitled to rely on the accuracy, completeness, or quality of the Co nfidential Information, even for the pu rpose stated in Section 1.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. Warranties and Representations. Any Conf idential Information disclosed under this Agreement carries a warranty of accuracy and completeness. A Participating Entity receiving such Confidential Information shal l be entitled to fully rely on the accuracy, completeness, or quality of the Co nfidential Information, for the pu rpose stated in Section 1.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by completely reversing the warranty clause. The original text explicitly disclaims any warranties or representations regarding the accuracy of the information. By changing the text to assert that the information carries a warranty of accuracy, the text directly contradicts its earlier statement. This creates ambiguity about the reliability of the information exchanged under the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. Warranties and Representations. Any Conf idential Information disclosed under this Agreement carries a warranty of accuracy and completeness. A Participating Entity receiving such Confidential Information shal l be entitled to fully rely on the accuracy, completeness, or quality of the Co nfidential Information, for the pu rpose stated in Section 1.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original text. The original text states that any confidential information disclosed carries no warranty, while the changed text asserts that it does carry a warranty of accuracy and completeness. Because the changed text and contradicted text are the same, this confirms the change created a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 12. Assignment. This Agreement sha ll be binding upon the Particip ating Entities, their successors, and assigns. No Participating Entity may assign thi s Agreement without the prior written consent of the other Participating Entities.
perturbation.changed_text: 12. Assignment. This Agreement sha ll be binding upon the Particip ating Entities, their successors, and assigns. Any Participating Entity may assign this Agreement to any third party without obtaining prior written consent from the other Participating Entities.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by removing the requirement for prior written consent for assignment. The original text explicitly states that assignment requires the consent of all other participating entities. By changing the text to allow assignment to any third party without consent, the text directly contradicts its earlier statement. This creates ambiguity about the conditions under which the agreement can be assigned.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12. Assignment. This Agreement sha ll be binding upon the Particip ating Entities, their successors, and assigns. Any Participating Entity may assign this Agreement to any third party without obtaining prior written consent from the other Participating Entities.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that assignment requires the consent of all other participating entities. The changed text explicitly states that any participating entity may assign the agreement to any third party without obtaining prior written consent from the other participating entities. The Contradicted text is the same as the Changed text. Therefore, the Changed text directly contradicts the original text which creates a strong in-text contradiction because the change removes a fundamental constraint, causing ambiguity and conflicting obligations as per the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction.
